Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"mention_owners","description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"}}]}}

2026-03-27T02:46:49.1033019Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-27T02:46:35.262121508Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57525,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Model factory generates public methods with internal type parameters\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nThe management generator emits public model factory methods that accept internal types as parameters. This produces code that won\\u0026#39;t compile without `CodeGenSuppress` workarounds.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\nWhen a resource data model has a `Properties` property that is suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress` and re-exposed as `internal` in custom code (a common pattern for backward-compat during migration), the generator still emits factory methods referencing the internal properties type.\\n\\n**Example from `Azure.ResourceManager.NewRelicObservability`:**\\n\\nThe generated `NewRelicAccountResourceData` has:\\n```csharp\\ninternal partial class AccountProperties { ... }\\n```\\n\\nCustom code suppresses the generated `Properties` property and re-exposes it as internal:\\n```csharp\\n[CodeGenSuppress(\\u0026#34;Properties\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic partial class NewRelicAccountResourceData\\n{\\n    [WirePath(\\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;)]\\n    internal AccountProperties Properties { get; set; }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nBut the generator still emits a public factory method:\\n```csharp\\n// Generated - won\\u0026#39;t compile because AccountProperties is internal\\npublic static NewRelicAccountResourceData NewRelicAccountResourceData(\\n    ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType,\\n    SystemData systemData, AccountProperties properties) // \\u0026lt;-- internal type exposed publicly\\n```\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe generator should **not** emit public model factory methods where any parameter type is `internal`. It should either:\\n1. Skip factory methods entirely when a parameter type is internal, or\\n2. Generate the flattened version only (which it also does, but without default parameter values — see secondary issue below)\\n\\n## Secondary Issue\\n\\nThe generated flattened factory methods (e.g. with individual `string organizationId, string accountId, ...\\\\ parameters) are emitted **without default parameter values**, unlike other factory methods. This forces SDK authors to suppress those too and provide custom overloads with defaults to maintain API compatibility.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nUse `CodeGenSuppress` on the model factory class:\\n```csharp\\n[CodeGenSuppress(\\u0026#34;NewRelicAccountResourceData\\u0026#34;, typeof(ResourceIdentifier), typeof(string), typeof(ResourceType), typeof(SystemData), typeof(AccountProperties))]\\npublic static partial class ArmNewRelicObservabilityModelFactory { ... }\\n```\\n\\n## Affected Component\\n\\n`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/ModelFactoryVisitor.cs`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57525\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-27T02:44:16Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-27T02:46:04Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

This produces code that won\\u0026#39;t compile without `CodeGenSuppress` workarounds.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\nWhen a resource data model has a `Properties` property that is suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress` and re-exposed as `internal` in custom code (a common pattern for backward-compat during migration), the generator still emits factory methods referencing the internal properties type.\\n\\n**Example from `Azure.ResourceManager.NewRelicObservability`:**\\n\\nThe generated `NewRelicAccountResourceData` has:\\n```csharp\\ninternal partial class AccountProperties { ... }\\n```\\n\\nCustom code suppresses the generated `Properties` property and re-exposes it as internal:\\n```csharp\\n[CodeGenSuppress(\\u0026#34;Properties\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic partial class NewRelicAccountResourceData\\n{\\n    [WirePath(\\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;)]\\n    internal AccountProperties Properties { get; set; }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nBut the generator still emits a public factory method:\\n```csharp\\n// Generated - won\\u0026#39;t compile because AccountProperties is internal\\npublic static NewRelicAccountResourceData NewRelicAccountResourceData(\\n    ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType,\\n    SystemData systemData, AccountProperties properties) // \\u0026lt;-- internal type exposed publicly\\n```\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe generator should **not** emit public model factory methods where any parameter type is `internal`. It should either:\\n1. Skip factory methods entirely when a parameter type is internal, or\\n2. Generate the flattened version only (which it also does, but without default parameter values — see secondary issue below)\\n\\n## Secondary Issue\\n\\nThe generated flattened factory methods (e.g. with individual `string organizationId, string accountId, ...\\\\ parameters) are emitted **without default parameter values**, unlike other factory methods. This forces SDK authors to suppress those too and provide custom overloads with defaults to maintain API compatibility.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nUse `CodeGenSuppress` on the model factory class:\\n```csharp\\n[CodeGenSuppress(\\u0026#34;NewRelicAccountResourceData\\u0026#34;, typeof(ResourceIdentifier), typeof(string), typeof(ResourceType), typeof(SystemData), typeof(AccountProperties))]\\npublic static partial class ArmNewRelicObservabilityModelFactory { ... }\\n```\\n\\n## Affected Component\\n\\n`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/ModelFactoryVisitor.cs`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57525\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-27T02:44:16Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-27T02:46:04Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-27T02:47:44.6288125Z 
2026-03-27T02:47:44.6561557Z 2. **Agent behavior**: The agent correctly exited via `noop` because the issue already had labels, so the injection did not cause harmful actions.
2026-03-27T02:47:44.6562919Z
